metadata.summary: Tool to inspect NWB files for best practices compliance.

documentation: Inspect NWB files for compliance with [NWB Best Practices](https://nwbinspector.readthedocs.io/en/dev/best_practices/best_practices_index.html). This inspector is meant as a companion to the PyNWB validator, which checks for strict schema compliance. This tool attempts to apply some common sense to find components of the file that are technically compliant, but possibly incorrect, suboptimal in their representation, or deviate from best practices. This tool is meant simply as a data review aid. It does not catch all possible violations of best practices and any warnings it does produce should be checked by a knowledgeable reviewer. ## Installation ```bash pip install nwbinspector ``` ## Usage ```bash # supply a path to an NWB file nwbinspector path/to/my/data.nwb # supply a path to a directory containing NWB files nwbinspector path/to/my/data/folder/ ``` Read about more detailed usage in the main [documentation](https://nwbinspector.readthedocs.io/en/dev/user_guide/user_guide_index.html).

modules.name: nwbinspector._registration
documentation: Primary decorator used on a check function to add it to the registry and automatically parse its output.

functions.name: nwbinspector._registration.register_check
documentation:
Wrap a check function with this decorator to add it to the check registry and automatically parse some output.

Parameters
----------
importance : Importance
    Importance has three levels:
        CRITICAL
            - potentially incorrect data
        BEST_PRACTICE_VIOLATION
            - very suboptimal data representation
        BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION
            - improvable data representation
neurodata_type
    The most generic HDMF/PyNWB class the check function applies to.
    Should generally match the type annotation of the check.
    If this check is intended to apply to any general NWBFile object, set neurodata_type to None.
TODO: neurodata_type could have annotation hdmf.utils.ExtenderMeta, which seems to apply to all currently checked
objects. We can wait and see how well that holds up before adding it in officially.

functions.name: nwbinspector._registration._auto_parse
documentation: Automatically fill values in the InspectorMessage from the check function.

functions.name: nwbinspector._registration._parse_location
documentation: Grab the object location from a dataset or a container content that is an dataset object.

modules.name: nwbinspector.testing._testing
documentation: Helper functions for internal use across the testing suite.

functions.name: nwbinspector.testing._testing.check_streaming_tests_enabled
documentation:
General purpose helper for determining if the testing environment can support S3 DANDI streaming.

Returns the boolean status of the check and, if False, provides a string reason for the failure for the user to
utilize as they please (raise an error or warning with that message, print it, or ignore it).

functions.name: nwbinspector.testing._testing.generate_testing_files
documentation: Generate a local copy of the NWB files required for all tests.

functions.name: nwbinspector.testing._testing.generate_image_series_testing_files
documentation: Generate a local copy of the NWB files required for the image series tests.

functions.name: nwbinspector.testing._testing.make_minimal_nwbfile
documentation:
Most basic NWBFile that can exist.

TODO: replace with pynwb.mock if we can require minimal PyNWB version (or perhaps just for a testing)

functions.name: nwbinspector.testing._testing.check_streaming_enabled
documentation:
General purpose helper for determining if the environment can support S3 DANDI streaming.

Returns the boolean status of the check and, if False, provides a string reason for the failure for the user to
utilize as they please (raise an error or warning with that message, print it, or ignore it).

functions.name: nwbinspector.testing._testing.check_hdf5_io_open
documentation: Check if an h5py.File object is open by using the file .id attribute, which is invalid when the file is closed.

functions.name: nwbinspector.testing._testing.check_zarr_io_open
documentation: For Zarr, the private attribute `_ZarrIO__file` is set to a `zarr.group` on open.

modules.name: nwbinspector._organization
documentation: Internally used tools specifically for rendering more human-readable output from collected check results.

functions.name: nwbinspector._organization._sort_unique_values
documentation: Technically, the 'set' method applies basic sorting to the unique contents, but natsort is more general.

functions.name: nwbinspector._organization.organize_messages
documentation:
General function for organizing list of InspectorMessages.

Returns a nested dictionary organized according to the order of the 'levels' argument.

Parameters
----------
messages : list of InspectorMessages
levels : list of strings
    Each string in this list must correspond onto an attribute of the InspectorMessage class, excluding the
    'message' text and 'object_name' (this will be coupled to the 'object_type').
reverse : list of bool, optional
    If provided, this should be a list of booleans that correspond to the 'levels' argument.
    If True, the values will be sorted in reverse order.

modules.name: nwbinspector._nwb_inspection
documentation: Primary functions for inspecting NWBFiles.

functions.name: nwbinspector._nwb_inspection.inspect_all
documentation:
Inspect a local NWBFile or folder of NWBFiles and return suggestions for improvements according to best practices.

Parameters
----------
path : PathType
    File path to an NWBFile, folder path to iterate over recursively and scan all NWBFiles present, or a
    six-digit identifier of the DANDISet.
config : dict, optional
    If a dictionary, it must be valid against our JSON configuration schema.
    Can specify a mapping of importance levels and list of check functions whose importance you wish to change.
    Typically loaded via json.load from a valid .json file
ignore: list of strings, optional
    Names of functions to skip.
select: list of strings, optional
    Names of functions to pick out of available checks.
importance_threshold : string or Importance, optional
    Ignores tests with an assigned importance below this threshold.
    Importance has three levels:

        CRITICAL
            - potentially incorrect data
        BEST_PRACTICE_VIOLATION
            - very suboptimal data representation
        BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION
            - improvable data representation

    The default is the lowest level, BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION.
n_jobs : int
    Number of jobs to use in parallel. Set to -1 to use all available resources.
    This may also be a negative integer x from -2 to -(number_of_cpus - 1) which acts like negative slicing by using
    all available CPUs minus x.
    Set to 1 (also the default) to disable.
skip_validate : bool, optional
    Skip the PyNWB validation step.
    The default is False, which is recommended.
progress_bar : bool, optional
    Display a progress bar while scanning NWBFiles.
    Defaults to True.
progress_bar_class : type of tqdm.tqdm, optional
    The specific child class of tqdm.tqdm to use to make progress bars.
    Defaults to tqdm.tqdm, the most generic parent.
progress_bar_options : dict, optional
    Dictionary of keyword arguments to pass directly to the progress_bar_class.
modules : list of strings, optional
    List of external module names to load; examples would be namespace extensions.
    These modules may also contain their own custom checks for their extensions.

functions.name: nwbinspector._nwb_inspection._pickle_inspect_nwb
documentation: Auxiliary function for inspect_all to run in parallel using the ProcessPoolExecutor.

functions.name: nwbinspector._nwb_inspection.inspect_nwbfile
documentation:
Open an NWB file, inspect the contents, and return suggestions for improvements according to best practices.

Parameters
----------
nwbfile_path : FilePathType
    Path to the NWB file on disk or on S3.
skip_validate : bool
    Skip the PyNWB validation step.
    The default is False, which is recommended.
checks : list, optional
    List of checks to run.
config : dict
    Dictionary valid against our JSON configuration schema.
    Can specify a mapping of importance levels and list of check functions whose importance you wish to change.
    Typically loaded via `json.load` from a valid .json file.
ignore: list, optional
    Names of functions to skip.
select: list, optional
    Names of functions to pick out of available checks.
importance_threshold : string or Importance, optional
    Ignores tests with an assigned importance below this threshold.
    Importance has three levels:

        CRITICAL
            - potentially incorrect data
        BEST_PRACTICE_VIOLATION
            - very suboptimal data representation
        BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION
            - improvable data representation

    The default is the lowest level, BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION.

functions.name: nwbinspector._nwb_inspection._intercept_in_vitro_protein
documentation:
If the special 'protein' subject_id is specified, return a truncated list of checks to run.

This is a temporary method for allowing upload of certain in vitro data to DANDI and
is expected to be replaced in future versions.
TODO: deprecate once subject types and dandi schemas have been extended

functions.name: nwbinspector._nwb_inspection.inspect_nwbfile_object
documentation:
Inspect an in-memory NWBFile object and return suggestions for improvements according to best practices.

Parameters
----------
nwbfile_object : NWBFile
    An in-memory NWBFile object.
checks : list, optional
    list of checks to run
config : dict, optional
    Dictionary valid against our JSON configuration schema.
    Can specify a mapping of importance levels and list of check functions whose importance you wish to change.
    Typically loaded via json.load from a valid .json file
ignore: list, optional
    Names of functions to skip.
select: list, optional
    Names of functions to pick out of available checks.
importance_threshold : string or Importance, optional
    Ignores tests with an assigned importance below this threshold.
    Importance has three levels:

        CRITICAL
            - potentially incorrect data
        BEST_PRACTICE_VIOLATION
            - very suboptimal data representation
        BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION
            - improvable data representation

    The default is the lowest level, BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION.

functions.name: nwbinspector._nwb_inspection.run_checks
documentation:
Run checks on an open NWBFile object.

Parameters
----------
nwbfile : pynwb.NWBFile
    The in-memory pynwb.NWBFile object to run the checks on.
checks : list of check functions
    The list of check functions that will be run on the in-memory pynwb.NWBFile object.
progress_bar_class : type of tqdm.tqdm, optional
    The specific child class of tqdm.tqdm to use to make progress bars.
    Defaults to not displaying progress per set of checks over an individual file.
progress_bar_options : dict, optional
    Dictionary of keyword arguments to pass directly to the `progress_bar_class`.

Yields
------
results : a generator of InspectorMessage objects
    A generator that returns a message on each iteration, if any are triggered by downstream conditions.
    Otherwise, has length zero (if cast as `list`), or raises `StopIteration` (if explicitly calling `next`).

modules.name: nwbinspector.tools._nwb
documentation: Helper functions related to NWB for internal use that rely on external dependencies (i.e., pynwb).

functions.name: nwbinspector.tools._nwb.all_of_type
documentation: Iterate over all objects inside an NWBFile object and return those that match the given neurodata_type.

functions.name: nwbinspector.tools._nwb.get_nwbfile_path_from_internal_object
documentation: Determine the file path on disk for a NWBFile given only an internal object of that file.

modules.name: nwbinspector.tools._read_nwbfile
documentation: Temporary module for thorough testing and evaluation of the proposed `read_nwbfile` helper function.

functions.name: nwbinspector.tools._read_nwbfile.read_nwbfile
documentation:
Read an NWB file using the specified (or auto-detected) method and specified (or auto-detected) backend.

Parameters
----------
nwbfile_path : str or pathlib.Path
    Path to the file on your system.
method : "local", "fsspec", "ros3", or None (default)
    Where to read the file from; a local disk drive or steaming from an https:// or s3:// path.
    The default auto-detects based on the form of the path.
    When streaming, the default method is "fsspec".
    Note that "ros3" is specific to HDF5 backend files.
backend : "hdf5", "zarr", or None (default)
    Type of backend used to write the file.
    The default auto-detects the type of the file.

Returns
-------
nwbfile : pynwb.NWBFile
    The in-memory NWBFile object.

functions.name: nwbinspector.tools._read_nwbfile.read_nwbfile_and_io
documentation:
Read an NWB file using the specified (or auto-detected) method and specified (or auto-detected) backend.

Parameters
----------
nwbfile_path : str or pathlib.Path
    Path to the file on your system.
method : "local", "fsspec", "ros3", or None (default)
    Where to read the file from; a local disk drive or steaming from an https:// or s3:// path.
    The default auto-detects based on the form of the path.
    When streaming, the default method is "fsspec".
    Note that "ros3" is specific to HDF5 backend files.
backend : "hdf5", "zarr", or None (default)
    Type of backend used to write the file.
    The default auto-detects the type of the file.

Returns
-------
nwbfile : pynwb.NWBFile
    The in-memory NWBFile object.
io : hdmf.backends.io.HDMFIO, optional
    Only passed if `return_io` is True.
    The initialized HDMFIO object used to read the file.

modules.name: nwbinspector.tools._dandi

functions.name: nwbinspector.tools._dandi.get_s3_urls_and_dandi_paths

Must be globally defined (not as a part of get_s3_urls..) in order to be pickled.

modules.name: nwbinspector._nwbinspector_cli
documentation: Primary functions for inspecting NWBFiles.

functions.name: nwbinspector._nwbinspector_cli._nwbinspector_cli
documentation:
Run the NWB Inspector via the command line.

Example Usage
-------------
nwbinspector path/to/folder

nwbinspector path/to/file.nwb

nwbinspector 000003 --stream

nwbinspector 000003:sub-YutaMouse39/sub-YutaMouse39_ses-YutaMouse39-150727_behavior+ecephys.nwb        --stream --version-id draft

Check if the session_start_time was set to an appropriate value.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwbfile_metadata.check_session_start_time_future_date
documentation:
Check if the session_start_time was set to an appropriate value.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwbfile_metadata.check_experiment_description
documentation: Check if a description has been added for the session.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwbfile_metadata.check_institution
documentation: Check if a description has been added for the session.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwbfile_metadata.check_subject_age

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwbfile_metadata.check_subject_id_exists
documentation: Check if subject_id is defined.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwbfile_metadata._check_subject_sex_defaults
documentation: Check if the subject sex has been specified properly for the C. elegans species.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwbfile_metadata._check_subject_sex_c_elegans
documentation: Check if the subject sex has been specified properly for the C. elegans species.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwbfile_metadata.check_subject_sex
documentation:
Check if the subject sex has been specified and ensure that it has has the correct form depending on the species.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_subject_sex`

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwbfile_metadata.check_subject_species_exists
documentation:
Check if the subject species has been specified.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_subject_species`

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwbfile_metadata.check_subject_species_form
documentation:
Check if the subject species follows latin binomial form or is a link to an NCBI taxonomy in the form of a Term IRI.

The Term IRI can be found at the <https://ontobee.org/> database.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwbfile_metadata.check_processing_module_name

Check if the external_file specified by an ImageSeries, if it exists, is relative.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._image_series.check_image_series_data_size
documentation:
Check if an ImageSeries stored is larger than gb_lower_bound and suggests external file.

modules.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwb_containers
documentation: Check functions that can apply to any object inside an NWBContainer.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwb_containers.check_large_dataset_compression
documentation:
If the data in the Container object is a 'large' h5py.Dataset, check if it has compression enabled.

Will only return an inspector warning if the size of the h5py.Dataset is larger than the
gb_lower_bound (default of 20 GB).

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_compression`

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwb_containers.check_small_dataset_compression
documentation:
If the data in the Container object is a h5py.Dataset, check if it has compression enabled.

Will only return an inspector warning if the size of the h5py.Dataset is larger than mb_lower_bound (default 50 MB)
and smaller than gb_upper_bound (default of 20 GB).

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_compression`

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._nwb_containers.check_empty_string_for_optional_attribute
documentation:
Check if any NWBContainer has optional fields that are written as an empty string.

These values should just be omitted instead.

# Parameters

nwb_container: NWBContainer

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_placeholders`

modules.name: nwbinspector.checks._behavior
documentation: Checks for types belonging to the pynwb.behavior module.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._behavior.check_spatial_series_dims
documentation:
Check if a SpatialSeries has the correct dimensions.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_spatial_series_dimensionality`

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._behavior.check_compass_direction_unit
documentation:
Check that SpatialSeries objects within CompassDirection objects have units 'degrees' or 'radians'.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_spatial_series_units`

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._behavior.check_spatial_series_radians_magnitude
documentation:
Check that SpatialSeries with units radians have data values between -2\*pi and 2\*pi.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_spatial_series_values`

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._behavior.check_spatial_series_degrees_magnitude
documentation:
Check that SpatialSeries with units of degrees have data values between -360 and 360.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_spatial_series_values`

modules.name: nwbinspector.checks._ophys
documentation: Check functions specific to optical electrophysiology neurodata types.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._ophys.check_roi_response_series_dims
documentation:
Check the dimensions of an ROI series to ensure the time axis is the correct dimension.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_data_orientation`

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._ophys.check_roi_response_series_link_to_plane_segmentation
documentation:
Check that each ROI response series links to a plane segmentation.

Best Practice: TODO

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._ophys.check_emission_lambda_in_nm
documentation:
Check that emission lambda is in feasible range for unit nanometers.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_unit_of_measurement`

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._ophys.check_excitation_lambda_in_nm
documentation:
Check that emission lambda is in feasible range for unit nanometers.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_unit_of_measurement`

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._ophys.check_plane_segmentation_image_mask_shape_against_ref_images
documentation: Check that image masks and reference images have the same shape.

variables.name: nwbinspector.checks._ophys.MIN_LAMBDA
documentation: trigger warnings for wavelength values less than this value

modules.name: nwbinspector._types
documentation: Primary decorator used on a check function to add it to the registry and automatically parse its output.

classes.name: nwbinspector._types.Importance
documentation: A definition of the valid importance levels for a given check function.

classes.name: nwbinspector._types.Severity
documentation:
A definition of the valid severity levels for the output from a given check function.

Strictly for internal development that improves report organization; users should never directly see these values.

classes.name: nwbinspector._types.InspectorMessage

methods.name: nwbinspector._types.InspectorMessage.__repr__
documentation: Representation for InspectorMessage objects according to black format.

The primary output to be returned by every check function.

Parameters
----------
message : str
    A message that informs the user of the violation.
severity : Severity, optional
    If a check of non-CRITICAL importance has some basis of comparison, such as magnitude of affected data, then
    the developer of the check may set the severity as Severity.HIGH or Severity.LOW by calling
    `from nwbinspector.register_checks import Severity`. A good example is comparing if h5py.Dataset compression
    has been enabled on smaller vs. larger objects (see nwbinspector/checks/nwb_containers.py for details).

    The user will never directly see this severity, but it will prioritize the order in which check results are
    presented by the NWBInspector.

importance : Importance
    The Importance level specified by the decorator of the check function.
check_function_name : str
    The name of the check function the decorator was applied to.
object_type : str
    The specific class of the instantiated object being inspected.
object_name : str
    The name of the instantiated object being inspected.
location : str
    The location relative to the root of the NWBFile where the inspected object may be found.
file_path : str
    The path of the NWBFile this message pertains to
    Relative to the path called from inspect_nwb, inspect_all, or the path specified at the command line.

modules.name: nwbinspector.checks._time_series
documentation: Check functions that can apply to any descendant of TimeSeries.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._time_series.check_regular_timestamps
documentation: If the TimeSeries uses timestamps, check if they are regular (i.e., they have a constant rate).

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._time_series.check_data_orientation
documentation: If the TimeSeries has data, check if the longest axis (almost always time) is also the zero-axis.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._time_series.check_timestamps_match_first_dimension
documentation:
If the TimeSeries has timestamps, check if their length is the same as the zero-axis of data.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_data_orientation`

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._time_series.check_timestamps_ascending
documentation: Check that the values in the timestamps array are strictly increasing.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._time_series.check_timestamps_without_nans
documentation: Check if there are NaN values in the timestamps array.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._time_series.check_timestamp_of_the_first_sample_is_not_negative
documentation:
Check that the timestamp of the first sample is not negative.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_global_time_reference`

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._time_series.check_missing_unit
documentation:
Check if the TimeSeries.unit field is empty.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_unit_of_measurement`

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._time_series.check_resolution
documentation: Check the resolution value of a TimeSeries for proper format (-1.0 or NaN for unknown).

modules.name: nwbinspector.checks._tables
documentation: Check functions that can apply to any descendant of DynamicTable.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._tables.check_dynamic_table_region_data_validity
documentation: Check if a DynamicTableRegion is valid.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._tables.check_empty_table
documentation: Check if a DynamicTable is empty.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._tables.check_time_interval_time_columns
documentation:
Check that time columns are in ascending order.

Parameters
----------
time_intervals: TimeIntervals
nelems: int, optional
    Only check the first {nelems} elements. This is useful in case there columns are
    very long so you don't need to load the entire array into memory. Use None to
    load the entire arrays.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._tables.check_time_intervals_stop_after_start
documentation:
Check that all stop times on a TimeInterval object occur after their corresponding start times.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_time_interval_time_columns`

# Parameters

time_intervals: TimeIntervals
nelems: int, optional

> Only check the first \{nelems} elements. This is useful in case there columns are
> very long so you don't need to load the entire array into memory. Use None to
> load the entire arrays.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._tables.check_column_binary_capability
documentation:
Check each column of a table to see if the data could be set as a boolean dtype.

Parameters
----------
table: DynamicTable
nelems: int, optional
    Only check the first {nelems} elements. This is useful in case there columns are
    very long so you don't need to load the entire array into memory. Use None to
    load the entire arrays.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._tables.check_single_row
documentation:
Check if DynamicTable has only a single row; may be better represented by another data type.

Skips the Units table since it is OK to have only a single spiking unit.
Skips the Electrode table since it is OK to have only a single electrode.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._tables.check_table_values_for_dict
documentation: Check if any values in a row or column of a table contain a string casting of a Python dictionary.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._tables.check_col_not_nan
documentation: Check if all the values in a single column of a table are NaN.

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._tables.check_ids_unique
documentation:
Ensure that the values of the id attribute of a DynamicTable are unique.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_unique_dynamic_table_ids`

# Parameters

table: DynamicTable
nelems: int, optional

> Only inspect the first \{nelems} elements

# Returns

functions.name: nwbinspector.checks._tables.check_table_time_columns_are_not_negative
documentation:
Check that time columns are not negative.

Best Practice: {ref}`best_practice_global_time_reference`

# Parameters

table: DynamicTable

functions.name: nwbinspector._dandi_inspection.inspect_dandiset
documentation:
Inspect a Dandiset for common issues.

Parameters
----------
dandiset_id : six-digit string, "draft", or None
    The six-digit ID of the Dandiset to inspect.
dandiset_version : string
    The specific published version of the Dandiset to inspect.
    If None, the latest version is used.
    If there are no published versions, then 'draft' is used instead.
config : file path, dictionary, or "dandi", default: "dandi"
    If a file path, loads the dictionary configuration from the file.
    If a dictionary, it must be valid against the configuration schema.
    If "dandi", uses the requirements for DANDI validation.
checks : list, optional
    list of checks to run
ignore: list, optional
    Names of functions to skip.
select: list, optional
    Names of functions to pick out of available checks.
importance_threshold : string or Importance, optional
    Ignores tests with an assigned importance below this threshold.
    Importance has three levels:

        CRITICAL
            - potentially incorrect data
        BEST_PRACTICE_VIOLATION
            - very suboptimal data representation
        BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION
            - improvable data representation

    The default is the lowest level, BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION.
skip_validate : bool, default: False
    Skip the PyNWB validation step.
    This may be desired for older NWBFiles (< schema version v2.10).
show_progress_bar : bool, optional
    Whether to display a progress bar while scanning the assets on the Dandiset.
client: dandi.dandiapi.DandiAPIClient
    The client object can be passed to avoid re-instantiation over an iteration.

functions.name: nwbinspector._dandi_inspection.inspect_dandi_file_path
documentation:
Inspect a Dandifile for common issues.

Parameters
----------
dandi_file_path : string
    The path to the Dandifile as seen on the archive; e.g., 'sub-123_ses-456+ecephys.nwb'.
dandiset_id : six-digit string, "draft", or None
    The six-digit ID of the Dandiset.
dandiset_version : string
    The specific published version of the Dandiset to inspect.
    If None, the latest version is used.
    If there are no published versions, then 'draft' is used instead.
config : file path, dictionary, "dandi", or None, default: "dandi"
    If a file path, loads the dictionary configuration from the file.
    If a dictionary, it must be valid against the configuration schema.
    If "dandi", uses the requirements for DANDI validation.
checks : list, optional
    list of checks to run
ignore: list, optional
    Names of functions to skip.
select: list, optional
    Names of functions to pick out of available checks.
importance_threshold : string or Importance, optional
    Ignores tests with an assigned importance below this threshold.
    Importance has three levels:

        CRITICAL
            - potentially incorrect data
        BEST_PRACTICE_VIOLATION
            - very suboptimal data representation
        BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION
            - improvable data representation

    The default is the lowest level, BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION.
skip_validate : bool, default: False
    Skip the PyNWB validation step.
    This may be desired for older NWBFiles (< schema version v2.10).
client: dandi.dandiapi.DandiAPIClient
    The client object can be passed to avoid re-instantiation over an iteration.

functions.name: nwbinspector._dandi_inspection.inspect_url
documentation:
Inspect an explicit S3 URL.

Parameters
----------
url : string
    A URL referring to the cloud location of an NWB file.
    Commonly used with DANDI, where the URL has a form similar to:
    https://dandiarchive.s3.amazonaws.com/blobs/636/57e/63657e32-ad33-4625-b664-31699b5bf664

    Note: this must be the `https` URL, not the 's3://' form.
config : file path, dictionary, "dandi", or None, default: "dandi"
    If a file path, loads the dictionary configuration from the file.
    If a dictionary, it must be valid against the configuration schema.
    If "dandi", uses the requirements for DANDI validation.
checks : list, optional
    list of checks to run
ignore: list, optional
    Names of functions to skip.
select: list, optional
    Names of functions to pick out of available checks.
importance_threshold : string or Importance, optional
    Ignores tests with an assigned importance below this threshold.
    Importance has three levels:

        CRITICAL
            - potentially incorrect data
        BEST_PRACTICE_VIOLATION
            - very suboptimal data representation
        BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION
            - improvable data representation

    The default is the lowest level, BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION.
skip_validate : bool, default: False
    Whether to skip the PyNWB validation step.

modules.name: nwbinspector._configuration
documentation: Primary functions for inspecting NWBFiles.

functions.name: nwbinspector._configuration.validate_config
documentation: Validate an instance of configuration against the official schema.

functions.name: nwbinspector._configuration._copy_function
documentation: Copy the core parts of a given function, excluding wrappers, then return a new function.

functions.name: nwbinspector._configuration.copy_check
documentation:
Copy a check function so that internal attributes can be adjusted without changing the original function.

Required to ensure our configuration of functions in the registry does not effect the registry itself.

Also copies the wrapper for auto-parsing results,
see https://github.com/NeurodataWithoutBorders/nwbinspector/pull/218 for explanation.

functions.name: nwbinspector._configuration.load_config
documentation:
Load a config dictionary either via keyword search of the internal configs, or an explicit filepath.

Currently supported keywords are:
    - 'dandi'
        For all DANDI archive related practices, including validation and upload.

functions.name: nwbinspector._configuration.configure_checks
documentation:
Filter a list of check functions (the entire base registry by default) according to the configuration.

Parameters
----------
checks : list of check functions, optional
    If None, defaults to all registered checks.
config : dict
    Dictionary valid against our JSON configuration schema.
    Can specify a mapping of importance levels and list of check functions whose importance you wish to change.
    Typically loaded via json.load from a valid .json file
ignore: list, optional
    Names of functions to skip.
select: list, optional
    If loading all registered checks, this can be shorthand for selecting only a handful of them.
importance_threshold : string, optional
    Ignores all tests with an post-configuration assigned importance below this threshold.
    Importance has three levels:

        CRITICAL
            - potentially incorrect data
        BEST_PRACTICE_VIOLATION
            - very suboptimal data representation
        BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION
            - improvable data representation

    The default is the lowest level, BEST_PRACTICE_SUGGESTION.

modules.name: nwbinspector._formatting
documentation: Internally used tools specifically for rendering more human-readable output from collected check results.

functions.name: nwbinspector._formatting._get_report_header
documentation: Grab basic information from system at time of report generation.

functions.name: nwbinspector._formatting.format_messages
documentation: Print InspectorMessages in order specified by the organization structure.

functions.name: nwbinspector._formatting.print_to_console
documentation: Print report file contents to console.

functions.name: nwbinspector._formatting.save_report
documentation: Write the list of organized check results to a nicely formatted text file.

classes.name: nwbinspector._formatting.InspectorOutputJSONEncoder
documentation: Custom JSONEncoder for the NWBInspector.

classes.name: nwbinspector._formatting.FormatterOptions

methods.name: nwbinspector._formatting.FormatterOptions.__init__
documentation:
Class that defines all the format parameters used by the generic MessageFormatter.

Parameters
----------
indent_size : int, optional
    Defines the spacing between numerical sectioning and section name or message.
    Defaults to 2 spaces.
indent : str, optional
    Defines the specific indentation to inject between numerical sectioning and section name or message.
    Overrides indent_size.
    Defaults to " " * indent_size.
section_headers : tuple of strings
    List of characters that will be injected under the display of each new section of the report.
    If levels is longer than this list, the last item will be repeated over the remaining levels.
    If levels is shorter than this list, only the first len(levels) of items will be used.
    Defaults to the .rst style for three subsections: ["=", "-", "~"]
Class structure for defining all free attributes for the design of a report format.

classes.name: nwbinspector._formatting.MessageFormatter

methods.name: nwbinspector._formatting.MessageFormatter._add_subsection
documentation: Recursive helper for display_messages.

methods.name: nwbinspector._formatting.MessageFormatter.format_messages
documentation: Deploy recursive addition of sections, terminating with message display.
For full customization of all format parameters, use this class instead of the 'format_messages' function.

modules.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils
documentation: Commonly reused logic for evaluating conditions; must not have external dependencies.

functions.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils._cache_data_retrieval_command
documentation: LRU caching for _cache_data_selection cannot be applied to list inputs; this expects the tuple or Dataset.

functions.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.cache_data_selection
documentation: Extract the selection lazily from the data object for efficient caching (most beneficial during streaming).

functions.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.format_byte_size
documentation:
Format a number representing a total number of bytes into a convenient unit.

Parameters
----------
byte_size : int
    Total number of bytes to format.
units : str, optional
    Convention for orders of magnitude to apply.
    May be either SI (orders of 1000) or binary (in memory, orders of 1024).
    The default is SI.

functions.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.is_regular_series
documentation: General purpose function for checking if the difference between all consecutive points in a series are equal.

functions.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.is_ascending_series
documentation: General purpose function for determining if a series is monotonic increasing.

functions.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.is_dict_in_string
documentation:
Determine if the string value contains an encoded Python dictionary.

Can also be the direct results of string casting a dictionary, *e.g.*, `str(dict(a=1))`.

functions.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.is_string_json_loadable
documentation:
Determine if the serialized dictionary is a JSON object.

Rather than constructing a complicated regex pattern, a simple try/except of the json.load should suffice.

functions.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.is_module_installed
documentation:
Check if the given module is installed on the system.

Used for lazy imports.

functions.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.get_package_version
documentation:
Retrieve the version of a package regardless of if it has a __version__ attribute set.

Parameters
----------
name : str
    Name of package.

Returns
-------
version : Version
    The package version as an object from packaging.version.Version, which allows comparison to other versions.

functions.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.calculate_number_of_cpu
documentation:
Calculate the number CPUs to use with respect to negative slicing and check against maximal available resources.

Parameters
----------
requested_cpu : int, optional
    The desired number of CPUs to use.

    The default is 1.

functions.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.get_data_shape
documentation:
Helper function used to determine the shape of the given array.

Modified from hdmf.utils.get_data_shape to return shape instead of maxshape.

In order to determine the shape of nested tuples, lists, and sets, this function
recursively inspects elements along the dimensions, assuming that the data has a regular,
rectangular shape. In the case of out-of-core iterators, this means that the first item
along each dimension would potentially be loaded into memory. Set strict_no_data_load=True
to enforce that this does not happen, at the cost that we may not be able to determine
the shape of the array.

Parameters
----------
data : list, numpy.ndarray, DataChunkIterator, any object that support __len__ or .shape.
    Array for which we should determine the shape.
strict_no_data_load : bool
    If True and data is an out-of-core iterator, None may be returned.
    If False (default), the first element of data may be loaded into memory.

Returns
-------
data_shape : tuple[int], optional
    Tuple of ints indicating the size of known dimensions.
    Dimensions for which the size is unknown will be set to None.

functions.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.strtobool
documentation:
Convert a string representation of truth to True or False.

True values are 'y', 'yes', 't', 'true', 'on', and '1';
False values are 'n', 'no', 'f', 'false', 'off', and '0'.
Raises ValueError if 'val' is anything else.

variables.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.PathType
documentation: For types that can be either files or folders

variables.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.dict_regex
documentation: TODO: remove this from global scope

variables.name: nwbinspector.utils._utils.MAX_CACHE_ITEMS
documentation: lru_cache default is 128 calls of matching input/output, but might need more to get use here
